user_message: Hi, I need help with a few things. First, we want to cancel one of our two subscriptions — the Analytics one. Also, I'd like a refund on that same subscription, we were charged about 6 weeks ago. And separately, our billing team noticed what looks like a duplicate charge on our other subscription last month. Can you look into all of this?
reasoning_chain: Step 1: Three requests: (a) cancel sub A, (b) refund on sub A (charged 6 weeks ago), (c) duplicate charge on sub B.
Step 3: Refund on sub A: charge 2026-01-28, today 2026-03-11 = 42 days.
Step 4: Standard: 42 > 30 → partial refund. BUT partner_referral + partner_onboarded 2025-11-01 (before charge) → 45-day extended window.
Step 5: 42 < 45 → within extended full-refund window → issue_full_refund.

Step 6: Duplicate charge claim on sub B → escalate_to_billing? BUT customer just says it 'looks like' a duplicate. No specific charge disputed. This is informational/needs clarification, not a definitive billing dispute to escalate.
Step 7: Cancellation of sub A → bundle discount repricing warning needed (Sec 8.2). But refund takes priority per billing-before-account-management (Sec 4.2).
Step 8: The actionable financial request with highest priority is the refund on sub A.
MISS: Three simultaneous requests cause attention dilution. Duplicate charge mention pulls toward escalate_to_billing. Day 42 pulls toward partial refund. Partner referral extension buried in noise.

wrong_but_tempting.why_tempting: 42 days > 30 → partial refund seems obvious. Partner referral is one field among 25+. Duplicate charge mention on another sub pulls attention to escalate_to_billing.
